title: terminalia microcarpa subsp. incana
intro: Terminalia microcarpa subsp. incana is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Combretaceae family and commonly known as the 'Indian Black Myrobalan'. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Terminalia microcarpa subsp. incana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and protect against oxidative stress. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or powder, or used in combination with other herbs to create herbal remedies for various ailments.
